code: @Manual{,   title = {SAMSE: South Atlantic Management Strategy Evaluation},   author = {Adrian Hordyk},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9007},   url = {https://safmc-mse.bluematterscience.com}, }

title: South Atlantic Management Strategy Evaluation
text: South Atlantic Fishery Management Council (SAFMC) working Blue Matter Science Ltd. conduct Management Strategy Evaluation (MSE) describe expected outcomes different management approaches Snapper-Grouper Fishery. SAMSE R package includes code run MSE analysis. SAMSE builds age-structured, spatial multispecies, operating models (OMs) output Beaufort Assessment Models (BAM). OMs projected forwarded different management scenarios performance statistics used compare performance different management approaches. SAMSE uses openMSE platform build OMs conduct closed-loop simulation testing.

title: South Atlantic Management Strategy Evaluation
text: can install SAMSE GitHub :

title: South Atlantic Management Strategy Evaluation
text: MSE Technical Specifications: document describing technical details MSE framework, including methodology assumptions creating operating models. Building Base Case Operating Model: article providing description reproducible code generating Base Case Operating Models. SAFMC MSE Results App: online App designed interactively explore MSE Results

what: Specifications for Importing BAM Assessments — BAM_Specs
title: Specifications for Importing BAM Assessments — BAM_Specs
text: BAM_Specs named list specifications importing BAM output operating model object (see MSEtool::OM()).

title: Specifications for Importing BAM Assessments — BAM_Specs
Specifications for Importing BAM Assessments — BAM_Specs
text: BAM_Specs named list, names correspond stock assessment available bamExtras package (.e., spaces name, see MSEtool::ListBAMStocks()). element BAM_Specs named list following named elements: Stock - character(1)name stock (typically name parentlist element, includes spaces) SEDAR - numeric(1)SEDAR assessment number. Optional. Link - URL SEDAR stock assessment report. Optional recommended. SurveyNames - character vector user-facing names survey indices detected BAMdata. Must length number survey indices found (.e. indices matched fleet name). Use override default names derived column names. UnitsLandings - character vector length equal number landings fleets detected. Required. element must one : "1000 lb" — values converted thousands pounds kg. "1000 n" — values multiplied 1,000 (number thousands). UnitsDiscards - character vector length equal number discard fleets detected. Accepts values UnitsLandings. DiscMortDF - data.frame columns Fleet, Value, Year specifying discard mortality rates fleet time block. Required cases. DiscFleets - Named character vector mapping retain fleet names corresponding discard fleet names BAM output, cases naming conventions differ. Required cases. DiscSelFleets - Named character vector mapping retain fleet names selectivity series use discards. Required cases. Required cases. RetSelFleets - Named character vector mapping retain fleet names alternative retention selectivity series. Required cases.

title: Specifications for Importing BAM Assessments — BAM_Specs
code: names(BAM_Specs) #>  [1] "BlackSeaBass"     "GagGrouper"       "GrayTriggerfish"  "GreaterAmberjack" #>  [5] "RedGrouper"       "RedPorgy"         "RedSnapper"       "ScampGrouper"     #>  [9] "SnowyGrouper"     "Tilefish"         "VermilionSnapper"  BAM_Specs[[1]] #> $Stock #> [1] "Black Sea Bass" #>  #> $SEDAR #> [1] 76 #>  #> $Link #> [1] "https://sedarweb.org/documents/sedar-76-stock-assessment-report-south-atlantic-black-sea-bass/" #>  #> $SurveyNames #> [1] "MARMAP blackfish/snapper trap" "SERFS Chevron Trap"            #>  #> $UnitsLandings #> [1] "1000 lb"  "1000 lb"  " 1000 lb" "1000 lb"  "1000 lb"  #>  #> $UnitsDiscards #> [1] "1000 lb" "1000 n"  "1000 n"  #>  #> $DiscMortDF #>   Fleet Value Year #> 1   cHL 0.190 1977 #> 2   cPT 0.140 1977 #> 3   cPT 0.068 2007 #> 4   rHB 0.152 1977 #> 5   rGN 0.137 1977 #>

title: Check Required Package Dependencies — CheckPackages
text: Validates required packages meet minimum version requirements. Throws informative error package missing date, including install command needed resolve issue.

title: Check Required Package Dependencies — CheckPackages
text: Invisibly returns NULL checks pass.

title: Check Required Package Dependencies — CheckPackages
text: following packages minimum versions checked:

title: Import BAM Operating Models — Import_OMs
text: Imports one stock Operating Models (OMs) BAM (Beaufort Assessment Model) output via MSEtool::ImportBAM(), optionally validates BAM population dynamics MSEtool::CompareBAM(), saves OM disk .om file.

title: Import BAM Operating Models — Import_OMs
code: Import_OMs(   Import_Stocks = NULL,   Compare = TRUE,   plot = FALSE,   OMpath = "Objects/OM/SingleStock" )

title: Import BAM Operating Models — Import_OMs
text: Import_Stocks character. Names stocks import. NULL (default), stocks defined BAM_Specs Compare logical. TRUE (default), runs MSEtool::CompareBAM() imported OM validate BAM reference points. plot logical. TRUE, diagnostic plots produced MSEtool::CompareBAM(). Passed directly function. Defaults FALSE. OMpath character. Directory .om files saved. Defaults "Objects/OM/SingleStock".

title: Import BAM Operating Models — Import_OMs
text: Called side effects. imported OM saved OMpath <StockName>.om. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Import BAM Operating Models — Import_OMs
text: Project-wide settings nSim, pYear) loaded start function via Settings(). stock's BAM configuration drawn BAM_Specs.

title: Manage Operating Model Files — OM-files
text: family functions listing, loading, deleting saved Operating Model (OM) files stored Objects/OM/ directory structure.

title: Manage Operating Model Files — OM-files
code: ListOMFiles(   OMpath = "Objects/OM",   type = c("SingleStock", "MultiStock"),   print = FALSE )  LoadOM(   name = NULL,   OMpath = "Objects/OM",   type = c("SingleStock", "MultiStock") )  DeleteOMs(OMpath = "Objects/OM", type = c("SingleStock", "MultiStock"))

title: Manage Operating Model Files — OM-files
text: OMpath character(1). Path directory containing OM subdirectories. Defaults "Objects/OM". type character(1). Type Operating Model. One "SingleStock" (default) "MultiStock". Determines subdirectory used within OMpath. print logical(1). TRUE, prints list found OM files console. FALSE (default), returns file paths invisibly without printing. name character(1). Name OM load, without .om extension. Defaults "BlackSeaBass". (LoadOM .)

title: Manage Operating Model Files — OM-files
text: ListOMFiles: character vector full file paths. Returned invisibly print = FALSE. LoadOM: OM object. DeleteOMs: logical vector base::file.remove(), one element per file, indicating whether deletion succeeded.

title: Open the SEDAR Stock Assessment Report for a Stock — SEDAR
text: Opens SEDAR stock assessment report given stock default web browser. Report URLs stored internal BAM_Specs list.

title: Open the SEDAR Stock Assessment Report for a Stock — SEDAR
Open the SEDAR Stock Assessment Report for a Stock — SEDAR
text: Stock character string giving stock name. Spaces ignored. Available stocks can viewed names(BAM_Specs).

what: Get or Set SAMSE Package Settings — Settings
title: Get or Set SAMSE Package Settings — Settings
text: Prints current package settings called arguments, updates one settings arguments supplied. Settings persist duration R session can set startup via .Rprofile (e.g. options(SAMSE.nSim = 100)).

title: Get or Set SAMSE Package Settings — Settings
Get or Set SAMSE Package Settings — Settings
text: ... Named arguments corresponding one settings update. Unrecognised names trigger error. Valid settings : nSim — number stochastic simulations (default: 50). pYear — number projection years (default: 30).

title: Get or Set SAMSE Package Settings — Settings
text: Invisibly returns named list current settings updates applied. called arguments, current settings also printed console.

title: Get or Set SAMSE Package Settings — Settings
code: # Print current settings Settings() #>  #> ── `SAMSE` Settings ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> nSim: 50 #> pYear: 30  # Update one setting Settings(nSim = 100)  # Update multiple settings at once Settings(nSim = 200, pYear = 50)

what: Fleet and Stock-Fleet Reference Tables — fleet-tables
title: Fleet and Stock-Fleet Reference Tables — fleet-tables
text: FleetTable() retrieves Operating Models via LoadOM(), extracts stock fleet names, returns tidy data frame joining fleet codes full names via FleetCodesTable. StockFleetTable() summarises collapsing stocks single comma-separated string per fleet.

title: Fleet and Stock-Fleet Reference Tables — fleet-tables
code: FleetTable(OM_List = NULL)  StockFleetTable(OM_List = NULL)  MissingFleets(OM_List = NULL)

title: Fleet and Stock-Fleet Reference Tables — fleet-tables
text: FleetTable(): data frame one row per stock-fleet combination columns: Stock: stock name derived first stock slot OM. Code: short fleet code (e.g. "cHL", "rHB"). Fleet: full fleet name looked FleetCodesTable. StockFleetTable(): data frame one row per fleet columns: Fleet: full fleet name. Stock: comma-separated string stocks associated fleet.
